name: Tool Directory Structure
description: Tools live in src/tools/<ToolName>/ directories. Each tool has self-contained implementation, input schema (Zod), permissions, and execution logic. Tool.ts (~29K lines) is the base.
parseTextToolCalls() in ollamaClient.ts didn't recognize <tool_call>[{name, arguments}]</tool_call> format used by fine-tuned models. Model output was treated as plain text instead of tool calls.
resolution: Added Pattern 3 to parseTextToolCalls() that extracts tool calls from <tool_call> blocks containing JSON arrays with {name, arguments} objects.

description: Ollama stream() suppressed native tools unless OLLAMA_NATIVE_TOOLS=true, while complete() always sent tools.
resolution: Use shared native-tool selection logic so both complete() and stream() auto-enable native tools for supported models, with explicit env override.

title: Skip remote managed settings in LOCAL_FIRST
rationale: When LOCAL_FIRST=true, startup should not treat placeholder or invalid Anthropic API keys as a reason to query the Anthropic managed-settings endpoint, because that stalls local-first startup before Ollama routing can run.

description: Interactive Docker UI debugging was mixed with local-first routing work, making it hard to separate REPL/TTY regressions from Ollama startup fixes.
resolution: Keep local-first startup fixes isolated from interactive terminal experiments; revert renderOptions/earlyInput/main interactive probes before the next UI debugging pass and retest from a clean baseline.

content: Training data tool descriptions must use runtime searchHint format (e.g. 'Read: read files, images, PDFs, notebooks') not short descriptions (e.g. 'Read file contents'). When OLLAMA_MINIMAL_TOOL_DESC=true, runtime uses 'ToolName: searchHint' format. Training script: docker_scripts/ollama/finetune/claude-explorer-model/v6/generate_v6_training.py WORKER_TOOLS dict.
